synopsis_understood.description: In 4-6 sentences, what is this product, who is it for, and what does it appear to do — based ONLY on what you saw publicly. Do not parrot the landing page; describe what you actually inferred from the brain outputs and report pages.

what_works.description: Concrete things the product does well. Each item must reference a specific URL or output you saw.

evidence_url.description: URL where you saw the evidence

evidence_quote.description: Verbatim quote from the page or caveat that proves the gap

data_gaps.description: Gaps you can prove from the product's own caveats and outputs. Mine the caveats lists in the brain outputs — they self-confess limitations. Each gap must have a verbatim quote.

topic.description: What kind of data is missing

why_it_matters.description: What operator question this would unlock

where_to_find_it.description: Specific public dataset name + URL pattern (e.g. 'Florida DOR sales tax monthly distributions at floridarevenue.com/Forms_library/...' — be specific, not generic)

missing_data_sources.description: Concrete public/affordable data sources this product should be ingesting but isn't. Be specific — name the agency, the dataset, the URL pattern. Vague answers like 'more economic data' are useless.

ux_problems.description: Broken links, jargon leaks (internal pack IDs leaking to users), placeholder titles, confusing nav, missing context, anything customer-hostile.

positioning_problems.description: Mismatches between what the landing page / connect page CLAIMS the product does and what the actual brain outputs DELIVER. Be specific about over-promises.

process_improvements.description: Suggestions for making the build pipeline, freshness, or output format better — judged from outside, not from inside.

questions_we_cannot_answer.description: Specific operator questions a Lee or Collier County prospector / broker / SMB owner would ask that the current brain set CANNOT answer. Frame as actual questions.
